subject: Mathematics
title: Escher's Mathematical Art
description: A quick, practical guide to Escher's Mathematical Art.

hook.text: M.C. Escher drew impossible staircases and interlocking lizards. His secret weapon was tessellation.

buildup.text: Escher modified basic tile shapes—squares, triangles, hexagons—into animals and figures that still fit together.

discovery.text: He used translation, rotation, and reflection to transform geometric tiles into living forms.

twist.text: Escher had no formal math training. Mathematicians later realized he had independently discovered deep symmetry concepts.

climax.text: His work is displayed in math departments worldwide—art that teaches geometry better than textbooks.

punchline.text: An artist with no math training produced profound mathematics.

quiz.question: What mathematical concept is central to Escher's art?
